title: Simultaneous first derivative spectrophotometric determination of copper(II) and nickel(II) using 1-phenyl-1,2-propanedione-2-oxime thiosemicarbazone
author: N. B. L. Prasad
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Oil Technological Research Institute, JNTU, Anantapur-515 001, Andhra Pradesh, India 

 Department of Chemistry, Sri Krishnadevaraya University, Anantapur-515 003, Andhra Pradesh. India 

  Manuscript received 15 July 2005, revised 27 January 2006. accepted 20 February 2006  

 The reagent, 1-phenyl-1,2-propanedione-2-oxime thiosemicarbazone (PPDOT) has been used for the determination of Cu ll  and Ni ll  by spectrophotometric method. The reagent gives intense colour reactions with copper(ll) and nickel(ll) in sodium acetate-acetic acid burrer medium. Maximum colour intensity is observed in the pH range 3.0-8.0. The molar absorptivity and Sandell's sensitivity of the methods for the determination of copper(ll) and nickel(ll) are 0.575 x 10 4 , 1.20 x 10 4  L mol -l  cm -1  and O.Oll8 and 0.00503 μg cm -2  respectively. The compositions of copper and nickel complexes of PPDOT as determined by Job's and molar ratio methods are found to be l : 2 (M : L). First derivative methods have been developed for the determination of Cu ll  and Ni ll.  Simultaneous spectrophotometric determination of Cu ll  and Ni ll  carried out by measuring the peak-base line technique at 500 and 467.5 nm respectively. The method is carried out without the need for employing simultaneous equations in the determination of Cu ll  and Ni ll  in synthetic mixtures and in edible oils and seeds.
